meta.title: RASY
meta.textPolicy: No HTML tags are stored in race/origin text fields. Text values are single-line strings; tables are stored as structured arrays.

origins.title: STARÁ KRV
origins.description: Sú pôvodnými obyvateľmi Axrie. Tvarovali históriu, stavali mestá a cesty, viedli medzi sebou vojny. Po svete sú rozšírené najviac a nájdu sa v každom hlavnom meste. Niekedy sú v mizivom počte ale nájdu sa ozaj v každom hlavnom meste.

origins.title: NOVÁ KRV
origins.description: Stará krv sa prelievala nie len v bojoch ale aj pri plodení. Tak vznikli mnohé nové rasy, niektoré sú výsledok len kombinovaním starej krvi no iný sú výsledok komplikovanejších kombinácií ako napríklad s návštevníkmi, čo je často krát skupiny rás, ktorá spája pôvodné rasy s inými svetmi. Zástupcov novej krvi existuje vo svete veľa. Majú svoje vlastné komunity no na rozdiel od starej krvi nie sú v každom hlavnom meste. Niekde je ich viac, ako napríklad Dworcov v Záskyni, a niekde nie sú vôbec, ako napríklad Dworcovia v Stromoraji.

origins.title: NÁVŠTEVNÍCI
origins.description: Tý, ktorý prišli z iných svetov. Mohli by sme ich deliť podľa svetov, podľa času kedy prišli alebo podľa ich vplyvu na svet ale nebudeme robiť nič z toho. Všetci sú totiž obyvatelia iných svetov a v Axrií sa ich nachádza len pár. Sú to prevažne dobrodruhovia, čierne ovce rodiny alebo oportunisti, ktorý sa z odlišných dôvodov vybrali žiť alebo putovať Axriou. Niektorý sú tu len pár dní, či mesiacov iný si tu založia nový život. V hlavných mestách ich nájsť len hŕstku. Niekde môžu mať menšie sídlo ale inde o nich nemuseli ani len počuť.

races.name: ELF
races.preview: Ancient, graceful people of the Old Blood, elves carry the memory of forests, coasts, deep seas, high halls, and shadowed paths. Their bloodlines make them adaptable wanderers, archers, mystics, climbers, or children of the tide.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Dex +2

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Although elves reach physical maturity at about the same age as humans, the elven understanding of adulthood goes beyond physical growth to encompass worldly experience. An elf typically claims adulthood and an adult name around the age of 100 and can live to be 750 years old.

items.label: Size
items.text: Elves range from under 5 to over 6 feet tall and have slender builds. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Darkvision
items.text: Accustomed to twilit forests and the night sky, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

items.label: Keen Senses
items.text: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

items.label: Fey Ancestry
items.text: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.

items.label: Trance
items.text: Elves don't need to sleep. Instead, they meditate deeply, remaining semiconscious, for 4 hours a day. (The Common word for such meditation is "Trance") While meditating, you can dream after a fashion; such dreams are actually mental exercises that have become reflexive through years of practice. After resting in this way, you gain the same benefit that a human does from 8 hours of sleep.
If you meditate during a long rest, you finish the rest after only 5 days instead of 7. You otherwise obey all the rules for a long rest; only the duration is changed.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Elvish. Elvish is fluid, with subtle intonations and intricate grammar. Elven literature is rich and varied, and their songs and poems are famous among other races. Many bards learn their language so they can add Elvish ballads to their repertoires.

items.label: Elfs are split into five sub-races
items.text: Coast, Drow, Forest, High and Sea.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Wis +1

items.label: Coast Elf Training
items.text: You have proficiency with the glaive, shortbow, longbow and net.

items.label: Underwater Darkvision
items.text: While you are submerged under water you can see in underwater dim light within 90 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in underwater darkness as if it were dim light.

items.label: Lungs of sea dog
items.text: When calculating how long you can hold your breath or not choke once you run out of breath you count your Constitution modifier twice.

items.label: Coasts sailor
items.text: You are proficient with vehicles (water)

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Cha +1

items.label: Superior Darkvision
items.text: Blessed by Vacan, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 120 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

items.label: Sunlight Sensitivity
items.text: You have disadvantage on attack rolls and on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight when you, the target of your attack, or whatever you are trying to perceive is in direct sunlight.

items.label: Drow Magic
items.text: You know the dancing lights cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the faerie fire spell once with this trait; you regain the ability to cast it when you finish a short rest (was long rest). When you reach 5th level, you can also cast the darkness spell once per day with this trait; you regain the ability to cast it when you finish a short rest (was long rest). Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

items.label: Drow Weapon Training
items.text: You have proficiency with rapiers, shortswords, and hand crossbows.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Str +1

items.label: Speed
items.text: Climb 30 ft

items.label: Forest Elf Training
items.text: You have proficiency with the longbow, oversized Longbow, whip and maul.

items.label: Natural climber
items.text: You have a climbing speed of 30 feet, you receive only half damage from fall damage

items.label: Long-Limbed
items.text: When you make a melee attack on your turn, your reach for it is 5 feet greater than normal.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Int +1

items.label: High Elf Weapon Training
items.text: You have proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.

items.label: Arcanist
items.text: You know one cantrip of your choice from the wizard spell list. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

items.label: Extra Language
items.text: You can speak, read, and write one extra language of your choosing.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Con +1

items.label: Speed
items.text: Swim 30 ft.

items.label: Sea Elf Training
items.text: You have proficiency with the spear, trident, light crossbow, and net.

items.label: Underwater Darkvision
items.text: While you are submerged under water you can see in underwater dim light within 120 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in underwater darkness as if it were dim light.

items.label: Child of the Sea
items.text: You have a swimming speed of 30 feet, and you can breathe air and water.

items.label: Friend of the Sea
items.text: Using gestures and sounds, you can communicate simple ideas with any beast that has an innate swimming Speed:

items.label: Extra Language
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Aquan.

races.name: GNOME
races.preview: Small in stature but enormous in wit, gnomes are clever makers, thinkers, and battlefield planners. Whether driven by discovery or strategy, they thrive when problems can be solved with invention, timing, or a sharp idea.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Int +2

items.label: Size
items.text: Small

items.label: Speed
items.text: 25 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Gnomes mature at the same rate humans do, and most are expected to settle down into an adult life by around age 40. They can live 350 to almost 500 years.

items.label: Size
items.text: Gnomes are between 3 and 4 feet tall and average about 40 pounds. Your size is Small.

items.label: Darkvision
items.text: Accustomed to life underground, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

items.label: Gnome Cunning
items.text: You have advantage on all Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws against magic.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Gnomish. The Gnomish language, which uses the Dwarvish script, is renowned for its technical treatises and its catalogs of knowledge about the natural world.

items.label: Gnomes are split into two sub-races
items.text: Discovery and Strategy.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Wis +1 or Cha +1

items.label: Artificer's Lore
items.text: Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to magic items, alchemical objects, or technological devices, you can add twice your proficiency bonus, instead of any proficiency bonus you normally apply.

items.label: Tinker
items.text: You have proficiency with artisan's tools (tinker's tools). Using those tools, you can spend 1 hour and 10 gp worth of materials to construct a Tiny clockwork device (AC 5, 1 hp). The device ceases to function after 24 hours (unless you spend 1 hour repairing it to keep the device functioning), or when you use your action to dismantle it; at that time, you can reclaim the materials used to create it. You can have up to three such devices active at a time.
When you create a device, choose one of the following options:

Clockwork Toy - This toy is a clockwork animal, monster, or person, such as a frog, mouse, bird, dragon, or soldier. When placed on the ground, the toy moves 5 feet across the ground on each of your turns in a random direction. It makes noises as appropriate to the creature it represents.
Fire Starter - The device produces a miniature flame, which you can use to light a candle, torch, or campfire. Using the device requires your action.
Music Box - When opened, this music box plays a single song at a moderate volume. The box stops playing when it reaches the song's end or when it is closed.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Str +1 or Dex +1

items.label: Ready to Rumble
items.text: You include your proficiency into your initiative rolls.

items.label: Powerful Build
items.text: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

items.label: Smither
items.text: When you spend a short rest attending to a weapon with which you are proficient with you will learn how to use it in the smartest way. This will increase its durability by 1 for you. (Noone else can benefit from this.) You can do this once per weapon. You can only have one weapon ready at time.

races.name: GOBLIN
races.preview: Fast, slippery, and far more dangerous than their size suggests, goblins survive by striking where larger folk are weakest. They are ideal for players who like mobility, mischief, and turning disadvantages into sudden opportunities.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Dex +3; Str -2; choose any other +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Small

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Creature Type
items.text: You are a Humanoid. You are also considered a goblinoid for any prerequisite or effect that requires you to be a goblinoid.

items.label: Darkvision
items.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were in dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

items.label: Fey Ancestry
items.text: You have advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the charmed condition on yourself.

items.label: Fury of the Small
items.text: When you damage a creature with an attack or a spell and the creature's size is larger than yours, you can cause the attack or spell to deal extra damage to the creature. The extra damage equals your proficiency bonus.
You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all expended uses when you finish a short rest (was long rest), and you can use it no more than once per turn.

items.label: Nimble Escape
items.text: You can take the Disengage or Hide action as a bonus action on each of your turns.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and one other language that you and your DM agree is appropriate for your character.

races.name: HUMAN
races.ability: Choose one of: (a) Str +1; Dex +1; Con +1; Int +1; Wis +1; Cha +1 (b) Choose any two +2
races.preview: Humans are the flexible heart of many realms: short-lived, ambitious, and endlessly varied. They fit almost any path, from common-born survivor to trained specialist, gifted prodigy, or determined adventurer.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Choose one of: (a) Str +1; Dex +1; Con +1; Int +1; Wis +1; Cha +1 (b) Choose any two unique +2

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Humans reach adulthood in their late teens and live less than a century.

items.label: Size
items.text: Humans vary widely in height and build, from barely 5 feet to well over 6 feet tall. Regardless of your position in that range, your size is Medium.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and one extra language of your choice. Humans typically learn the languages of other peoples they deal with, including obscure dialects. They are fond of sprinkling their speech with words borrowed from other tongues: Orc curses, Elvish musical expressions, Dwarvish military phrases, and so on.

items.label: Skills
items.text: You gain proficiency in one skill of your choice.
Applies only if variant (b) is chosen:

items.label: Feat
items.text: You gain one feat of your choice.

races.name: ORC
races.preview: Strong, direct, and brutally resilient, orcs carry a fierce Old Blood legacy shaped by endurance and conflict. Their earth, sand, and rock branches lean into aggression, momentum, survival, and refusing to fall.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Str +3; Int -2; choose any other +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Orcs reach adulthood at age 12 and live up to 70 years.

items.label: Darkvision
items.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

items.label: Powerful Build
items.text: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

items.label: Relentless Endurance
items.text: When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 hit point instead. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a short rest (was long rest).

items.label: Primal Intuition
items.text: You have proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Animal Handling, Insight, Intimidation, Medicine, Nature, Perception, and Survival.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and one other language that you and your DM agree is appropriate for your character.

items.label: Orcs are split into three sub-races
items.text: Earth, Sand and Rock.

items.label: Aggressive
items.text: As a bonus action, you can move up to your movement speed toward a hostile creature you can see or hear. You must end this move closer to the enemy than you started.

items.label: Blood bath
items.text: Whenever you strike an enemy down (either kill it or incapacitate it), you gain a number of temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus. You then also have advantage on your next attack roll.

items.label: Adrenaline Rush
items.text: You can take the Dash action as a bonus action. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a short rest (was long rest).
Whenever you use this trait, you gain a number of temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus.

items.label: Endurance
items.text: Rock orcs are known for their remarkable endurance and ability to withstand harsh conditions. You have advantage on saving throws against exhaustion.

items.label: Tough as Hill
items.text: Whenever you gain temporary hit points, the amount of temporary hit points you receive is increased by an additional 50% (rounded up). For example, if you would gain 11 temporary hit points, you gain 17 instead. (11+5.5)

races.name: DWARF
races.preview: Dwarves are sturdy folk of stone, craft, memory, and stubborn survival. Their sub-races reflect the work of their halls: brewers, forgers, miners, and jewelers, each turning tradition into a practical strength.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Con +2

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 25 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Dwarves mature at the same rate as humans, but they're considered young until they reach the age of 50. On average, they live about 350 years.

items.label: Size
items.text: Dwarves stand between 4 and 5 feet tall and average about 150 pounds. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Speed
items.text: Your speed is not reduced by wearing heavy armor.

items.label: Darkvision
items.text: Accustomed to life underground, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

items.label: Dwarven Resilience
items.text: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage

items.label: Dwarven Combat Training
items.text: You have proficiency with the battleaxe, handaxe, light hammer, and warhammer.

items.label: Stonecunning
items.text: Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to the origin of stonework, you are considered proficient in the History skill and add double your proficiency bonus to the check, instead of your normal proficiency bonus.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Dwarvish. Dwarvish is full of hard consonants and guttural sounds, and those characteristics spill over into whatever other language a dwarf might speak.

items.label: Dwarfs are split into four sub-races
items.text: Brewer, Forger, Miner and Jeweler.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Int +1

items.label: Tool Proficiency
items.text: You gain proficiency with the Brewer's supplies.

items.label: Brewers Toughness
items.text: Your hit point maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 every time you gain a level.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Dex +1

items.label: Tool Proficiency
items.text: You gain proficiency with the Smith's tools.

items.label: Heat of the Forge
items.text: You gain resistance to Fire damage

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Str +1

items.label: Tool Proficiency
items.text: You gain proficiency with the Mason's tools.

items.label: Superior Darkvision
items.text: Accustomed to life underground, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 120 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

items.label: Miners Magic
items.text: When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the Enlarge/Reduce spell on yourself once with this trait, using only the spell's enlarge option. When you reach 5th level, you can cast the Fortune's Favor spell on yourself once with this trait. You don't need material components for either spell, and you can't cast them while you're in direct sunlight, although sunlight has no effect on them once cast. You regain the ability to cast these spells with this trait when you finish a short rest (was long rest). Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

items.label: Sunlight Sensitivity
items.text: You have disadvantage on attack rolls and Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight when you, the target of your attack, or whatever you are trying to perceive is in direct sunlight.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Cha +1

items.label: Tool Proficiency
items.text: You gain proficiency with the Jeweler's tools.

items.label: Craft and Sell
items.text: You gain proficiency with two skills: Sleight of Hand and Persuasion.

races.name: HALFLING
races.preview: Halflings are small, lucky, and easy to underestimate, which is often their greatest advantage. Their branches favor nimble travel, social charm, or quiet cleverness touched by simple practical magic.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Dex +2

items.label: Size
items.text: Small

items.label: Speed
items.text: 25 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: A halfling reaches adulthood at the age of 20 and generally lives into the middle of his or her second century.

items.label: Size
items.text: Halflings average about 3 feet tall and weigh about 40 pounds. Your size is Small.

items.label: Lucky
items.text: When you roll a 1 on an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll.

items.label: Brave
items.text: You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

items.label: Halfling Nimbleness
items.text: You can move through the space of any creature that is of a size larger than yours.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Halfling. The Halfling language isn't secret, but halflings are loath to share it with others. They write very little, so they don't have a rich body of literature. Their oral tradition, however, is very strong. Almost all halflings speak Common to converse with the people in whose lands they dwell or through which they are traveling.

items.label: Halflings are split into three sub-races
items.text: Of Big foot, Of Charming tongue, Of Pure mind .

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Wis +1

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Natural to Stealth
items.text: You can attempt to hide even when you are obscured only by a creature that is at least one size larger than you.

items.label: Big step for Halfling
items.text: You can travel a bit faster than other halflings, you have a speed of 30 ft. instead of 25 ft.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Cha +1

items.label: Voice of people
items.text: You are proficient with two skills: Performance and Deception.

items.label: Way with words
items.text: When you use your Lucky feature for skill using Charisma you roll with advantage. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all expended uses when you finish a short rest (was long rest), and you can use it no more than once per turn.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Int +1

items.label: Travelers knowledge
items.text: You know the prestidigitation cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the identify spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a short rest (was long rest). When you reach 5th level, you can cast the See Invisibility spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a short rest (was long rest). Casting these spells with this trait doesn't require material components. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

races.name: AASIMAR
races.ability: Cha +2; and choose one (a) Str +1 (b) Wis +1 (c) Con +1
races.preview: Aasimar carry a celestial spark inside mortal blood, shining brightest when danger forces their true nature into the open. They are natural beacons, healers, avengers, or radiant terrors depending on how that light manifests.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Cha +2; and choose one (a) Str +1 (b) Wis +1 (c) Con +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Aasimar mature at the same rate as humans, but they can live up to 160 years.

items.label: Size
items.text: Aasimar have the same range of height and weight as humans.

items.label: Darkvision
items.text: Blessed with a radiant soul, your vision can easily cut through darkness. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

items.label: Celestial Resistance
items.text: You have resistance to necrotic damage and radiant damage.

items.label: Healing Hands
items.text: As an action, you can touch a creature and cause it to regain a number of hit points equal to your level. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short rest (was long rest).

items.label: Light Bearer
items.text: You know the light cantrip. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Celestial.

items.label: Celestial Revelation
items.text: When you reach 3rd level, choose one of the revelation options below. Thereafter, you can use a bonus action to unleash the celestial energy within yourself, gaining the benefits of that revelation. Your transformation lasts for 1 minute or until you end it as a bonus action. Once you transform using your revelation below, you can't use it again until you finish a short rest (was long rest).

items.label: Necrotic Shroud
items.text: Your eyes briefly become pools of darkness, and ghostly, flightless wings sprout from your back temporarily. Creatures other than your allies within 10 feet of you that can see you must succeed on a Charisma saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier) or become frightened of you until the end of your next turn. Until the transformation ends, once on each of your turns, you can deal extra necrotic damage to one target when you deal damage to it with an attack or a spell. The extra damage equals your proficiency bonus.

items.label: Radiant Consumption
items.text: Searing light temporarily radiates from your eyes and mouth. For the duration, you shed bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet, and at the end of each of your turns, each creature within 10 feet of you takes radiant damage equal to your proficiency bonus. Until the transformation ends, once on each of your turns, you can deal extra radiant damage to one target when you deal damage to it with an attack or a spell. The extra damage equals your proficiency bonus.

items.label: Radiant Soul
items.text: Two luminous, spectral wings sprout from your back temporarily. Until the transformation ends, you have a flying speed equal to your walking speed, and once on each of your turns, you can deal extra radiant damage to one target when you deal damage to it with an attack or a spell. The extra damage equals your proficiency bonus.

races.name: DRAKARIM
races.preview: Drakarim are marked by distant but powerful draconic ancestry, combining mortal versatility with elemental force. Their chosen dragon blood shapes their resistance, damage, and the way they expose enemies to ruin.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Str +2; Cha +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Drakarim mature at roughly the same rate as humans and reach adulthood in their late teens. Owing to their draconic heritage, they can live up to 150 years.

items.label: Size
items.text: Drakarim are broadly human in shape, though often taller and more powerfully built. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic.

items.label: Draconic Ancestry
items.text: You have a distant but potent draconic lineage. Choose one type of dragon from the Draconic Ancestry table. Your draconic ancestry determines the damage type of your draconic traits and the saving throw associated with them.

items.label: Draconic Mark
items.text: You can momentarily expose a creature’s defenses with a surge of draconic energy.

As a bonus action, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The target must make a saving throw determined by your Draconic Ancestry (DC = 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus).
On a failed save, the target takes 1d6 damage of the type associated with your draconic ancestry, and then becomes vulnerable to that damage type until the end of your next turn.
On a successful save, the target takes 1d4 damage of that type and suffers no additional effects.
The damage increases to 2d6 (or 2d4) at 5th level, 3d6 (or 3d4) at 11th level, and 4d6 (or 4d4) at 17th level.

A creature that is immune to the damage type is unaffected by this trait. If the creature has resistance to the damage type, that resistance is suppressed for the duration of the vulnerability instead of becoming vulnerability.
Once you use this trait, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

items.label: Draconic Resilience
items.text: You have resistance to the damage type associated with your Draconic Ancestry.

items.label: Human Versatility
items.text: You gain one feat of your choice.

races.name: DWORC
races.ability: Choose one (a) Str +2; Con +1 or (b) Str +1; Con +2
races.preview: Dworcs blend dwarven toughness with orcish physical power, creating a compact but formidable people built for hardship. They suit characters who value endurance, strength, survival instincts, and old grudges carried in the bones.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Choose one (a) Str +2; Con +1 or (b) Str +1; Con +2

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 25 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Dworcs mature at the same rate as dwarfs (so as humans), but they're considered young until they reach the age of 35. On average, they live about 125 years.

items.label: Size
items.text: Borks are usually around 5.5 feet tall and weigh between 180 and 230 pounds. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Darkvision
items.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

items.label: Dworcs Resilience
items.text: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage

items.label: Powerful Build
items.text: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

items.label: Primal Intuition
items.text: You have proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Animal Handling, Insight, Intimidation, Medicine, Nature, Perception, and Survival.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common, Orc and Dwarvish.

races.name: GENASI
races.preview: Genasi are mortal bodies stirred by elemental power, each branch expressing a different force of the world. Air, earth, fire, lightning, and water genasi all feel distinct, giving this race a strong identity through chosen element.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Con +2

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Genasi mature at about the same rate as humans and reach adulthood in their late teens. They live somewhat longer than humans do, up to 120 years.

items.label: Alignment
items.text: Independent and self-reliant, genasi tend toward a chaotic alignment.

items.label: Size
items.text: Genasi are as varied as their mortal parents but are generally built like humans, standing anywhere from 5 feet to over 6 feet tall. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Primordial. Primordial is a guttural language, filled with harsh syllables and hard consonants.

items.label: Genasi are split into five sub-races
items.text: Air, Earth, Fire, Lightning and Water

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Dex +1

items.label: Unending Breath
items.text: You can hold your breath indefinitely while you're not incapacitated.

items.label: Mingle with the Wind
items.text: You can cast the levitate spell once with this trait, requiring no material components, and you regain the ability to cast it this way when you finish a short rest (was long rest). Constitution is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Str +1

items.label: Earth Walk
items.text: You can move across difficult terrain made of earth or stone without expending extra movement.

items.label: Merge with Stone
items.text: You can cast the pass without trace spell once with this trait, requiring no material components, and you regain the ability to cast it this way when you finish a short rest (was long rest). Constitution is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Int +1

items.label: Darkvision
items.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. Your ties to the Elemental Plane of Fire make your darkvision unusual: everything you see in darkness is in a shade of red.

items.label: Fire Resistance
items.text: You have resistance to fire damage

items.label: Reach to the Blaze
items.text: You know the produce flame cantrip. Once you reach 3rd level, you can cast the burning hands spell once with this trait as a 1st-level spell, and you regain the ability to cast it this way when you finish a short rest (was long rest). Constitution is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Cha +1

items.label: Tireless
items.text: Your long rests require only half of normal resting time. You finish the long rest after only 4 days instead of 6. You otherwise obey all the rules for a long rest; only the duration is changed.

items.label: Lightning rod
items.text: You have resistance to Lightning and Thudner damage

items.label: Storm on horizon
items.text: . You can cast the Blur to go to page 3 spell once with this trait, requiring no material components, and you regain the ability to cast it this way when you finish a short rest (was long rest). Constitution is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Wis +1

items.label: Speed
items.text: swim 30 ft.

items.label: Acid Resistance
items.text: You have resistance to acid damage

items.label: Amphibious
items.text: You can breathe air and water.

items.label: Swim
items.text: You have a swimming speed of 30 feet.

items.label: Call to the Wave
items.text: You know the shape water cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the create or destroy water spell as a 2nd-level spell once with this trait, and you regain the ability to cast it this way when you finish a short rest (was long rest). Constitution is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

races.name: HALF-ELF
races.ability: Cha +2; Choose any other two unique +1
races.preview: Half-elves stand between human adaptability and elven legacy, often belonging everywhere and nowhere at once. Their variant legacies let them inherit gifts from different elven bloodlines while keeping broad personal freedom.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Cha +2; Choose any other two unique +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Half-elves mature at the same rate humans do and reach adulthood around the age of 20. They live much longer than humans, however, often exceeding 180 years.

items.label: Size
items.text: Half-elves are about the same size as humans, ranging from 5 to 6 feet tall. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Darkvision
items.text: Thanks to your elf blood, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

items.label: Fey Ancestry
items.text: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.

items.label: Variant feature
items.text: Choose one.

items.label: (Human legacy) Skill Versatility
items.text: You gain proficiency in two skills of your choice.

items.label: (Coast legacy) Lungs of sea dog
items.text: When calculating how long you can hold your breath or not choke once you run out of breath you count your Constitution modifier twice.

items.label: (Drow legacy) Drow Magic
items.text: You know the dancing lights cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the faerie fire spell once with this trait; you regain the ability to cast it when you finish a short rest (was long rest). When you reach 5th level, you can also cast the darkness spell once per day with this trait; you regain the ability to cast it when you finish a short rest (was long rest). Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

items.label: (Forest legacy) Natural climber
items.text: You have a climbing speed of 30 feet, you receive only half damage from fall damage

items.label: (High legacy) Arcanist
items.text: You know one cantrip of your choice from the wizard spell list. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

items.label: (Sea legacy) Underwater Darkvision
items.text: While you are submerged under water you can see in underwater dim light within 120 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in underwater darkness as if it were dim light.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common, Elvish, and one extra language of your choice.

races.name: HALF-ORC
races.preview: Half-orcs combine human drive with orcish force, making them fierce survivors who thrive under pressure. Their legacy options let them lean into brutal strikes, sudden rushes, endurance, or the savage edge of battle.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Str +2; Con +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Half-orcs mature a little faster than humans, reaching adulthood around age 14. They age noticeably faster and rarely live longer than 75 years.

items.label: Size
items.text: Half-orcs are somewhat larger and bulkier than humans, and they range from 5 to well over 6 feet tall. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Darkvision
items.text: Thanks to your orc blood, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

items.label: Menacing
items.text: You gain proficiency in the Intimidation skill.

items.label: Relentless Endurance
items.text: When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 hit point instead. You can't use this feature again until you finish a short rest (was long rest).

items.label: Variant feature
items.text: Choose one.

items.label: (Human legacy) Savage Attacks
items.text: When you score a critical hit with a melee weapon attack, you can roll one of the weapon's damage dice one additional time and add it to the extra damage of the critical hit.

items.label: (Earth legacy) Blood bath
items.text: Whenever you strike an enemy down (either kill it or incapacitate it), you gain a number of temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus. You then also have advantage on your next attack roll.

items.label: (Sand legacy) Adrenaline Rush
items.text: You can take the Dash action as a bonus action. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a short rest (was long rest). Whenever you use this trait, you gain a number of temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus.

items.label: (Rock legacy) Endurance
items.text: You have advantage on saving throws against exhaustion.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Orc. Orc is a harsh, grating language with hard consonants. It has no script of its own but is written in the Dwarvish script.

races.name: HALKEN
races.preview: Halkens are thoughtful descendants of human and vedalken heritage, marked by calm minds and careful precision. They suit characters who solve danger through reason, focus, skill, and a touch of amphibious otherworldliness.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Int +2; Wis +1 or Cha +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Halkens mature at a pace between that of humans and Vedalken, reaching adulthood in their mid-20s. They have a lifespan of about 150 years, though some live up to 200 years.

items.label: Alignment
items.text: Halkens are versatile like humans but tend to lean towards lawful tendencies due to their Vedalken heritage. They can be of any alignment.

items.label: Size
items.text: Halkens range from 5½ to 6½ feet tall and have a slender build. Their size is Medium.

items.label: Caution thinker
items.text: You have advantage on Intelligence saving throws and on saving throws against being charmed.

items.label: Partially Amphibious Ancestry
items.text: By absorbing oxygen through your skin, you can breathe underwater for up to 30 minutes. Once you've reached that limit, you can't use this trait again until you finish a short rest (was long rest).

items.label: Tireless Precision
items.text: You are proficient in one of the following skills of your choice: Arcana, History, Investigation, Medicine, Performance, or Sleight of Hand. You are also proficient with one tool of your choice.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common, Vedalken, and one additional language of your choice.

races.name: HOBGOBLIN
races.preview: Hobgoblins bring discipline, martial focus, and a retaliatory edge to the New Blood. They make excellent tactical warriors, armored casters, or hard-minded soldiers who punish enemies for underestimating them.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Con +2; Int +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Hobgoblins mature at the same rate as humans and have lifespans similar in length to theirs.

items.label: Size
items.text: Your size is Medium.

items.label: Darkvision
items.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

items.label: Martial Training
items.text: You are proficient with two martial weapons of your choice and with light armor.

items.label: Fury of the Goblin
items.text: When a medium or larger size creature which you already damaged attacks you and deals damage to you. You can use your reaction and attack it back. You can use, weapon attack or cantrip.
You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all expended uses when you finish a short rest (was long rest), and you can use it no more than once per turn.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Goblin.

races.name: TIEFLING
races.preview: Tieflings bear infernal influence in their blood, gaining fire, darkness, and a presence that is hard to ignore. Some carry an even stronger beastlike inheritance, trading comfort near holy places for claws, tail, and wings.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Cha +2; Int +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Tieflings mature at the same rate as humans but live a few years longer.

items.label: Size
items.text: Tieflings are about the same size and build as humans. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Darkvision
items.text: Thanks to your infernal heritage, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

items.label: Hellish Resistance
items.text: You have resistance to fire damage

items.label: Infernal Legacy
items.text: You know the thaumaturgy cantrip. Once you reach 3rd level, you can cast the hellish rebuke spell as a 2nd-level spell with this trait; you regain the ability to cast it when you finish a short rest (was long rest). Once you reach 5th level, you can also cast the darkness spell once per day with this trait; you regain the ability to cast it when you finish a short rest (was long rest). Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Infernal.

items.label: Nature of the Beast
items.text: You inherited more than most of the tieflings, your blood is more 'pure'. You have disadvantage on attack rolls and saving throws while close to holy items (radius 90 ft.) or while in location touched by good or neutral deities. (Like Churches, Cemeteries, Druid grows, Chapels, Altars, Ritual sites.. etc.). On the other side you have Claws and Tail.

items.label: Claws and Tail
items.text: You can use your claws to make unarmed strikes. When you hit with them, the strike deals 1d6 + your Strength modifier slashing damage, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike. You can use your tail to hold one not heavy item, such as a potion or dagger.

items.label: Wings of Hades
items.text: Once you reach 3rd level, you grow bat-like wings which grant you 30 ft flying speed. Once you reach 9th level, your wings grow larger which grant you 60 ft flying speed.

races.name: AARAKOCRA
races.preview: Aarakocra are swift skyborne visitors, light-bodied and born to think vertically. Their flight, talons, and wind-touched nature make them ideal scouts, messengers, hunters, or untouchable ranged fighters.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Dex +2; Wis +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 25 ft., fly 50 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Aarakocra reach maturity by age 3. Compared to humans, aarakocra don't usually live longer than 30 years.

items.label: Alignment
items.text: Most aarakocra are good and rarely choose sides when it comes to law and chaos. Tribal leaders and warriors might be lawful, while explorers and adventurers might tend toward chaotic.

items.label: Size
items.text: Aarakocra are about 5 feet tall. They have thin, lightweight bodies that weigh between 80 and 100 pounds. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Flight
items.text: You have a flying speed of 50 feet. To use this speed, you can't be wearing medium or heavy armor.

items.label: Talons and Beak
items.text: Your talons and beak are natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, you deal slashing damage equal to 1d4 + your Strength modifier, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

items.label: Language
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common, Aarakocra, and Auran.

races.name: BUGBEAR
races.preview: Bugbears are long-limbed ambushers with surprising stealth for their size. They hit hard from unexpected reach, carry more than they should, and reward players who enjoy opening combat with a nasty surprise.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Str +2; Dex +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Bugbears reach adulthood at age 16 and live up to 80 years.

items.label: Size
items.text: Bugbears are between 6 and 8 feet tall and weigh between 250 and 350 pounds. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Darkvision
items.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

items.label: Long-Limbed
items.text: When you make a melee attack on your turn, your reach for it is 5 feet greater than normal.

items.label: Powerful Build
items.text: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

items.label: Sneaky
items.text: You are proficient in the Stealth skill.

items.label: Surprise Attack
items.text: If you surprise a creature and hit it with an attack on your first turn in combat, the attack deals an extra 2d6 damage to it. You can use this trait only once per combat.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Goblin.

Long-Limbed When you make a melee attack on your turn, your reach for it is 5 feet greater than normal. Powerful Build You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift. Sneaky You are proficient in the Stealth skill. Surprise Attack If you surprise a creature and hit it with an attack on your first turn in combat, the attack deals an extra 2d6 damage to it. You can use this trait only once per combat. Languages You can speak, read, and write Common and Goblin.

races.name: CHANGELING
races.preview: Changelings are masters of identity, voice, and appearance, slipping through society by becoming whoever the moment requires. They shine in intrigue, deception, diplomacy, and stories where truth is a mask.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Cha +2; Choose any other +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Changelings mature slightly faster than humans but share a similar lifespan—typically a century or less. While a changeling can transform to conceal their age, the effects of aging affect them similarly to humans.

items.label: Alignment
items.text: Changelings tend toward pragmatic neutrality, and few changelings embrace evil.

items.label: Size
items.text: Your size is Medium.

items.label: Shapechanger
items.text: As an action, you can change your appearance and your voice. You determine the specifics of the changes, including your coloration, hair length, and sex. You can also adjust your height and weight, but not so much that your size changes. You can make yourself appear as a member of another race, though none of your game statistics change. You can't duplicate the appearance of a creature you've never seen, and you must adopt a form that has the same basic arrangement of limbs that you have. Your clothing and equipment aren't changed by this trait.
You stay in the new form until you use an action to revert to your true form or until you die.

items.label: Changeling Instincts
items.text: You gain proficiency with two of the following skills of your choice: Deception, Insight, Intimidation, and Persuasion.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and two other languages of your choice.

races.name: DRAGONBORN
races.preview: Dragonborn are proud draconic visitors whose ancestry determines their breath and resistance. They are bold, elemental, and direct, suited for characters who want their heritage to announce itself in battle.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Str +2; Cha +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Young dragonborn grow quickly. They walk hours after hatching, attain the size and development of a 10-year-old human child by the age of 3, and reach adulthood by 15. They live to be around 80.

items.label: Size
items.text: Dragonborn are taller and heavier than humans, standing well over 6 feet tall and averaging almost 250 pounds. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Draconic Ancestry
items.text: You have draconic ancestry. Choose one type of dragon from the Draconic Ancestry table. Your breath weapon and damage resistance are determined by the dragon type, as shown in the table.

items.label: Breath Weapon
items.text: You can use your action to exhale destructive energy. Your draconic ancestry determines the size, shape, and damage type of the exhalation.

When you use your breath weapon, each creature in the area of the exhalation must make a saving throw, the type of which is determined by your draconic ancestry. The DC for this saving throw equals 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus. A creature takes 2d6 damage on a failed save, and half as much damage on a successful one. The damage increases to 3d6 at 6th level, 4d6 at 11th level, and 5d6 at 16th level.
After you use your breath weapon, you can't use it again until you complete a short or long rest.

items.label: Damage Resistance
items.text: You have resistance to the damage type associated with your draconic ancestry.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic. Draconic is thought to be one of the oldest languages and is often used in the study of magic. The language sounds harsh to most other creatures and includes numerous hard consonants and sibilants.

races.name: FIRBOLG
races.preview: Firbolgs are gentle giants touched by fey magic and deep natural instinct. They prefer subtlety over spectacle, using hidden steps, quiet spells, and a bond with beasts and plants to avoid needless violence.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Wis +2; Str +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: As humanoids related to the fey, firbolg have long lifespans. A firbolg reaches adulthood around 30, and the oldest of them can live for 500 years.

items.label: Size
items.text: Firbolg are between 7 and 8 feet tall and weigh between 240 and 300 pounds. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Firbolg Magic
items.text: You can cast detect magic and disguise self with this trait, using Wisdom as your spellcasting ability for them. Once you cast either spell, you can't cast it again with this trait until you finish a short or long rest. When you use this version of disguise self, you can seem up to 3 feet shorter than normal, allowing you to more easily blend in with humans and elves.

items.label: Hidden Step
items.text: As a bonus action, you can magically turn invisible until the start of your next turn or until you attack, make a damage roll, or force someone to make a saving throw. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

items.label: Powerful Build
items.text: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

items.label: Speech of Beast and Leaf
items.text: You have the ability to communicate in a limited manner with beasts and plants. They can understand the meaning of your words, though you have no special ability to understand them in return. You have advantage on all Charisma checks you make to influence them.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common, Elvish, and Giant.

races.name: GOLIATH
races.preview: Goliaths are mountain-born survivors built for endurance, contests, and harsh terrain. Their strength, cold resistance, and ability to shrug off injury make them feel like living stone on the battlefield.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Str +2; Con +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Goliaths have lifespans comparable to humans. They enter adulthood in their late teens and usually live less than a century.

items.label: Size
items.text: Goliaths are between 7 and 8 feet tall and weigh between 280 and 340 pounds. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Natural Athlete
items.text: You have proficiency in the Athletics skill.

items.label: Stone's Enduranc
items.text: . You can focus yourself to occasionally shrug off injury. When you take damage, you can use your reaction to roll a d12. Add your Constitution modifier to the number rolled, and reduce the damage by that total. After you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

items.label: Powerful Build
items.text: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

items.label: Mountain Born
items.text: You have resistance to cold damage You're also acclimated to high altitude, including elevations above 20,000 feet.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Giant.

races.name: HARENGON
races.preview: Harengon are quick-footed wanderers with sharp senses and explosive movement. They are excellent for players who enjoy initiative, lucky escapes, sudden repositioning, and a character who is always hard to pin down.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Dex +2; Int +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Small or Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Size
items.text: You are Medium or Small. You choose the size when you select this race.

items.label: Hare-Trigger
items.text: You can add your proficiency bonus to your initiative rolls.

items.label: Leporine Senses
items.text: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

items.label: Lucky Footwork
items.text: When you fail a Dexterity saving throw, you can use your reaction to roll a d4 and add it to the save, potentially turning the failure into a success. You can't use this reaction if you're prone or your speed is 0.

items.label: Rabbit Hop
items.text: As a bonus action, you can jump a number of feet equal to five times your proficiency bonus, without provoking opportunity attacks. You can use this trait only if your speed is greater than 0. You can use it a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a short rest (was long rest).

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and one other language that you and your DM agree is appropriate for your character.

races.name: KOBOLD
races.preview: Kobolds are small, clever, and dangerous when fighting with allies. They rely on pack tactics, dirty tricks, and desperate courage, making them perfect for players who enjoy teamwork and tactical mischief.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Str -2; Dex +2; Int +2

items.label: Size
items.text: Small

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Kobolds reach adulthood at age 6 and can live up to 120 years but rarely do so.

items.label: Size
items.text: Kobolds are between 2 and 3 feet tall and weigh between 25 and 35 pounds. Your size is Small.

items.label: Darkvision
items.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

items.label: Grovel, Cower, and Beg
items.text: As an action on your turn, you can cower pathetically to distract nearby foes. Until the end of your next turn, your allies gain advantage on attack rolls against enemies within 10 feet of you that you can see. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

items.label: Pack Tactics
items.text: You have advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of your allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

items.label: Sunlight Sensitivity
items.text: You have disadvantage on attack rolls and on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight when you, the target of your attack, or whatever you are trying to perceive is in direct sunlight.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic.

races.name: LIZARDFOLK
races.preview: Lizardfolk are practical, predatory survivors shaped by water, teeth, hide, and instinct. They bring natural armor, a vicious bite, hunting skills, and a coldly useful approach to the world around them.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Con +2; Wis +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft., swim 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Lizardfolk reach maturity around age 14 and rarely live longer than 60 years.

items.label: Size
items.text: Lizardfolk are a little bulkier and taller than humans, and their colorful frills make them appear even larger. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Swim Speed
items.text: You have a swimming speed of 30 feet.

items.label: Bite
items.text: Your fanged maw is a natural weapon, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with it, you deal piercing damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

items.label: Cunning Artisan
items.text: As part of a short rest, you can harvest bone and hide from a slain beast, construct, dragon, monstrosity, or plant creature of size small or larger to create one of the following items: a shield, a club, a javelin, or 1d4 darts or blowgun needles. To use this trait, you need a blade, such as a dagger, or appropriate artisan's tools, such as leatherworker's tools.

items.label: Hold Breath
items.text: You can hold your breath for up to 15 minutes at a time.

items.label: Hunter's Lore
items.text: You gain proficiency with two of the following skills of your choice: Animal Handling, Nature, Perception, Stealth, and Survival.

items.label: Natural Armor
items.text: You have tough, scaly skin. When you aren't wearing armor, your AC is 13 + your Dexterity modifier. You can use your natural armor to determine your AC if the armor you wear would leave you with a lower AC. A shield's benefits apply as normal while you use your natural armor.

items.label: Tip
items.text: AC Calculations Don't Stack

- When the game gives you more than one way to calculate your Armor Class, you can use only one of them. You choose the one to use. For example, if you have the Natural Armor trait and the monk's Unarmored Defense feature, you don't mix them together. Instead, you choose which one determines your AC.

items.label: Hungry Jaws
items.text: In battle, you can throw yourself into a vicious feeding frenzy. As a bonus action, you can make a special attack with your bite. If the attack hits, it deals its normal damage, and you gain temporary hit points equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1), and you can't use this trait again until you finish a short or long rest.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic.

races.name: LOXODON
races.preview: Loxodons are calm, massive, and deeply grounded people with powerful bodies and steady spirits. Their thick skin, trunk, keen smell, and serenity make them protective companions and memorable gentle giants.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Con +2; Wis +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Loxodons physically mature at the same rate as humans, but they live about 450 years. They highly value the weight of wisdom and experience and are considered young until they reach the age of 60.

items.label: Alignment
items.text: Most loxodons are lawful, believing in the value of a peaceful, ordered life. They also tend toward good.

items.label: Size
items.text: Loxodons stand between 7 and 8 feet tall. Their massive bodies weigh between 300 and 400 pounds. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Powerful Build
items.text: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

items.label: Loxodon Serenity
items.text: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened.

items.label: Natural Armor
items.text: You have thick, leathery skin. When you aren't wearing armor, your AC is 12 + your Constitution modifier. You can use your natural armor to determine your AC if the armor you wear would leave you with a lower AC. A shield's benefits apply as normal while you use your natural armor.

items.label: Tip
items.text: AC Calculations Don't Stack

- When the game gives you more than one way to calculate your Armor Class, you can use only one of them. You choose the one to use. For example, if you have the Natural Armor trait and the monk's Unarmored Defense feature, you don't mix them together. Instead, you choose which one determines your AC.

items.label: Trunk
items.text: You can grasp things with your trunk, and you can use it as a snorkel. It has a reach of 5 feet, and it can lift a number of pounds equal to five times your Strength score. You can use it to do the following simple tasks: lift, drop, hold, push, or pull an object or a creature; open or close a door or a container; grapple someone; or make an unarmed strike. Your DM might allow other simple tasks to be added to that list of options.
Your trunk can't wield weapons or shields or do anything that requires manual precision, such as using tools or magic items or performing the somatic components of a spell.

items.label: Keen Smell
items.text: Thanks to your sensitive trunk, you have advantage on Wisdom (Perception), Wisdom (Survival), and Intelligence (Investigation) checks that involve smell.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Loxodon.

races.name: MINOTAUR
races.preview: Minotaurs are imposing horned warriors built for momentum and confrontation. Their charges, shoves, and natural weapons make them feel physical, aggressive, and impossible to ignore in close combat.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Str +2; Con +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Minotaurs mature and age at about the same rate as humans.

items.label: Size
items.text: Minotaurs average over 6 feet in height, and they have stocky builds. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Horns
items.text: Your horns are natural melee weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, you deal piercing damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

items.label: Goring Rush
items.text: Immediately after you use the Dash action on your turn and move at least 20 feet, you can make one melee attack with your horns as a bonus action.

items.label: Hammering Horns
items.text: Immediately after you hit a creature with a melee attack as part of the Attack action on your turn, you can use a bonus action to attempt to shove that target with your horns. The target must be no more than one size larger than you and within 5 feet of you. Unless it succeeds on a Strength saving throw against a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier, you push it up to 10 feet away from you.

items.label: Imposing Presence
items.text: You have proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Intimidation or Persuasion.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and Minotaur.

races.name: SHIFTER
races.preview: Shifters carry a bestial spirit under the skin, revealing it in short bursts of instinct and power. Each form changes the feel of the race, from armored endurance to fanged attacks, swift movement, or hunter’s focus.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: See ASI from Shifting

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Bestial Instincts
items.text: Channeling the beast within, you have proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Acrobatics, Athletics, Intimidation, or Survival.

items.label: Darkvision
items.text: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were in dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

items.label: Shifting
items.text: As a bonus action, you can assume a more bestial appearance. This transformation lasts for 1 minute, until you die, or until you revert to your normal appearance as a bonus action. When you shift, you gain temporary hit points equal to 2 × your proficiency bonus. You can shift a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a short rest (was long rest).
Whenever you shift, you gain an additional benefit based on one of the following options (choose when you select this race):

items.label: Beasthide
items.text: You gain 1d6 additional temporary hit points. While shifted, you have a +1 bonus to your Armor Class. Ability Scores: Con +2; Str +1

items.label: Longtooth
items.text: When you shift and as a bonus action on your other turns while shifted, you can use your elongated fangs to make an unarmed strike. If you hit with your fangs, you can deal piercing damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike. Ability Scores: Str +2; Dex +1

items.label: Swiftstride
items.text: While shifted, your walking speed increases by 10 feet. Additionally, you can move up to 10 feet as a reaction when a creature ends its turn within 5 feet of you. This reactive movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks. Ability Scores: Dex +2; Cha +1

items.label: Wildhunt
items.text: While shifted, you have advantage on Wisdom checks, and no creature within 30 feet of you can make an attack roll with advantage against you unless you're incapacitated. Ability Scores: Wis +2; Dex +1

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and one other language that you and your DM agree is appropriate for your character.

races.name: TABAXI
races.preview: Tabaxi are agile catfolk driven by speed, curiosity, and sharp senses. With climbing claws, sudden bursts of movement, and natural stealth, they excel as scouts, skirmishers, thieves, and restless wanderers.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Dex +2; Cha +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft., climb 20 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Tabaxi have lifespans equivalent to humans.

items.label: Size
items.text: Tabaxi are taller on average than humans and relatively slender. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Darkvision
items.text: You have a cat's keen senses, especially in the dark. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

items.label: Feline Agility
items.text: Your reflexes and agility allow you to move with a burst of speed. When you move on your turn in combat, you can double your speed until the end of the turn. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you move 0 feet on one of your turns.

items.label: Cat's Claws
items.text: Because of your claws, you have a climbing speed of 20 feet. In addition, your claws are natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, you deal slashing damage equal to 1d4 + your Strength modifier, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

items.label: Cat's Talents
items.text: You have proficiency in the Perception and Stealth skills.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and one other language of your choice.

races.name: TORTLE
races.preview: Tortles are wandering shell-bearers who carry their home and defense with them. Their natural armor, claws, breath control, and survival instincts make them steady adventurers with a calm but stubborn presence.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Str +2; Wis +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Young tortles crawl for a few weeks after birth before learning to walk on two legs. They reach adulthood by the age of 15 and live an average of 250 years.

items.label: Alignment
items.text: Tortles tend to lead orderly, ritualistic lives. They develop customs and routines, becoming more set in their ways as they age. Most are lawful good. A few can be selfish and greedy, tending more toward evil, but it's unusual for a tortle to shuck off order in favor of chaos.

items.label: Size
items.text: Tortle adults stand 5 to 6 feet tall and average 450 pounds. Their shells account for roughly one-third of their weight. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Claws
items.text: Your claws are natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, you deal slashing damage equal to 1d4 + your Strength modifier, instead of bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

items.label: Hold Breath
items.text: You can hold your breath for up to 1 hour at a time. Tortles aren't natural swimmers, but they can remain underwater for some time before needing to come up for air.

items.label: Natural Armor
items.text: Due to your shell and the shape of your body, you are ill-suited to wearing armor. Your shell provides ample protection, however; it gives you a base AC of 17 (your Dexterity modifier doesn't affect this number). You gain no benefit from wearing armor, but if you are using a shield, you can apply the shield's bonus as normal.

items.label: Shell Defense
items.text: You can withdraw into your shell as an action. Until you emerge, you gain a +4 bonus to AC, and you have advantage on Strength and Constitution saving throws. While in your shell, you are prone, your speed is 0 and can't increase, you have disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws, you can't take reactions, and the only action you can take is a bonus action to emerge from your shell.

items.label: Survival Instinct
items.text: You gain proficiency in the Survival skill. Tortles have finely honed survival instincts.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Aquan and Common.

If you hit with them, you deal slashing damage equal to 1d4 + your Strength modifier, instead of bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike. Hold Breath You can hold your breath for up to 1 hour at a time. Tortles aren't natural swimmers, but they can remain underwater for some time before needing to come up for air. Natural Armor Due to your shell and the shape of your body, you are ill-suited to wearing armor. Your shell provides ample protection, however; it gives you a base AC of 17 (your Dexterity modifier doesn't affect this number). You gain no benefit from wearing armor, but if you are using a shield, you can apply the shield's bonus as normal. Shell Defense You can withdraw into your shell as an action. Until you emerge, you gain a +4 bonus to AC, and you have advantage on Strength and Constitution saving throws. While in your shell, you are prone, your speed is 0 and can't increase, you have disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws, you can't take reactions, and the only action you can take is a bonus action to emerge from your shell. Survival Instinct You gain proficiency in the Survival skill. Tortles have finely honed survival instincts. Languages You can speak, read, and write Aquan and Common.

races.name: VEDALKEN
races.preview: Vedalken are analytical, composed visitors who trust precision more than impulse. Their mental discipline, chosen expertise, and limited underwater breathing make them excellent scholars, investigators, and controlled problem-solvers.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Int +2; Wis +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: Vedalken mature slower than humans do, reaching maturity around age 40. Their life span is typically 350 years, with some living to the age of 500.

items.label: Alignment
items.text: Vedalken are usually lawful and non-evil.

items.label: Size
items.text: Tall and slender, Vedalken stand 6 to 6½ feet tall on average and usually weigh less than 200 pounds. Your size is Medium.

items.label: Vedalken Dispassion
items.text: You have advantage on all Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws.

items.label: Tireless Precision
items.text: You are proficient in one of the following skills of your choice: Arcana, History, Investigation, Medicine, Performance, or Sleight of Hand. You are also proficient with one tool of your choice.
Whenever you make an ability check with the chosen skill or tool, roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the check's total.

items.label: Partially Amphibious
items.text: By absorbing oxygen through your skin, you can breathe underwater for up to 1 hour. Once you've reached that limit, you can't use this trait again until you finish a short rest (was long rest).

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common, Vedalken, and one other language of your choice.

races.name: WARFORGED
races.preview: Warforged are constructed beings searching for purpose beyond the reason they were made. Durable, tireless, and customizable, they fit stories of duty, identity, memory, and becoming more than a weapon.

items.label: Ability Scores
items.text: Con +2; Choose any other +1

items.label: Size
items.text: Medium

items.label: Speed
items.text: 30 ft.

items.label: Age
items.text: A typical warforged is between two and thirty years old. The maximum warforged lifespan remains a mystery; so far, warforged have shown no signs of deterioration due to age. You are immune to magical aging effects.

items.label: Alignment
items.text: Most warforged take comfort in order and discipline, tending toward lawful. But some have absorbed the morality, or lack thereof, of the beings with which they served.

items.label: Size
items.text: Your size is Medium.

items.label: Constructed Resilience
items.text: You were created to have remarkable fortitude, represented by the following benefits:
- You have advantage on saving throws against being poisoned, and you have resistance to poison damage
- You don't need to eat, drink, or breathe.
- You are immune to disease.
- You don't need to sleep, and magic can't put you to sleep.

items.label: Sentry's Rest
items.text: When you take a long rest, you must spend at least six hours in an inactive, motionless state, rather than sleeping. In this state, you appear inert, but it doesn't render you unconscious, and you can see and hear as normal.

items.label: Integrated Protection
items.text: Your body has built-in defensive layers, which can be enhanced with armor:
- You gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.

- You can don only armor with which you have proficiency. To don armor other than a shield, you must incorporate it into your body over the course of 1 hour, during which you remain in contact with the armor. To doff armor, you must spend 1 hour removing it. You can rest while donning or doffing armor in this way.
- While you live, the armor incorporated into your body can't be removed against your will.

items.label: Specialized Design
items.text: You gain one skill proficiency and one tool proficiency of your choice.

items.label: Languages
items.text: You can speak, read, and write Common and one other language of your choice.
